2604.18226.title: Model in Distress: Sentiment Analysis on French Synthetic Social Media
2604.18226.paper_summary: Automated analysis of customer feedback on social media is hindered by three challenges: the high cost of annotated training data, the scarcity of evaluation sets, especially in multilingual settings, and privacy concerns that prevent data sharing and reproducibility. We address these issues by developing a generalizable synthetic data generation pipeline applied to a case study on customer distress detection in French public transportation. Our approach utilizes backtranslation with fine-tuned models to generate 1.7 million synthetic tweets from a small seed corpus, complemented by synthetic reasoning traces. We train 600M-parameter reasoners with English and French reasoning that achieve 77-79% accuracy on human-annotated evaluation data, matching or exceeding SOTA proprietary LLMs and specialized encoders. Beyond reducing annotation costs, our pipeline preserves privacy by eliminating the exposure of sensitive user data. Our methodology can be adopted for other use cases and languages.
2604.18226.paper_summary_zh: 自動化分析社交媒體上的客戶反饋面臨三個挑戰：註釋訓練數據的高成本、評估集的稀缺，特別是在多語言環境中，以及阻止數據共享和可重現性的隱私問題。<lb/>我們通過開發一個可泛化的合成數據生成管道來解決這些問題，並將其應用於法國公共交通中客戶困擾檢測的案例研究。<lb/>我們的方法利用反向翻譯和微調模型，從一個小的種子語料庫生成170萬條合成推文，並輔以合成推理痕跡。<lb/>我們訓練了600M參數的推理器，使用英語和法語推理，在人類註釋的評估數據上達到77-79%的準確率，與SOTA專有LLM和專門編碼器相匹配或超過。<lb/>除了降低註釋成本外，我們的管道還通過消除敏感用戶數據的暴露來保護隱私。<lb/>我們的方法論可以應用於其他用例和語言。
2604.18226.author: Pierre-Carl Langlais et.al.
2604.18226.authors: Pierre-Carl Langlais, Pavel Chizhov, Yannick Detrois, Carlos Rosas Hinostroza, Ivan P. Yamshchikov, Bastien Perroy
